observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
Local Chief Executive (LCE) reconstitute an Inventory/Appraisal/Disposal Committee to conduct a complete annual physical inventory in order to (i) identify unserviceable or fully-depreciated properties for disposal; (ii) provide appraisal value for properties needed to be recorded in the books in the absence of any valid acquisition documents as basis for costing; and (iii) make available all necessary reports that perennially hindered the discovery of any discrepancies/errors between the records kept by the Accounting Office and the MTO; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the  Inventory/Appraisal/Disposal Committee: (i) maintain the required documents such as PPELCs and Property Cards; (ii) exert more effort to secure documents such as deeds of donation from the donors of donated properties, proofs of ownership for untitled lands, and the like to warrant the existence and validity of the recorded PPE accounts; and (iii) make a periodic reconciliation of records to immediately detect errors/irregularities.

We recommended that the management instruct the:
Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to properly obligate payments of programs/projects based on fulfilled contracts or delivered services for accuracy of recording and reporting; and
Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries to recognize three completed projects under the Continuing Appropriations in the total amount of ₱2,526,502.09.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more diligence in analyzing the prior years’ balances of ₱249,403.62 and conduct immediate reconciliation with the GSIS to determine the actual and correct balance of the Due to GSIS account.

We recommended and management agreed that:
refrain from granting cash advances for types of expenses not falling within the scope and definition of cash advances as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and discontinue the practice of procuring goods and repairing vehicles thru cash advances; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances within the reglementary period; (ii) stop the practice of granting new cash advances without the liquidation of previous ones; (iii) ensure that all grants and liquidations of cash advances are supported with complete documentations.

We recommended that management (a) require concerned members of the Bids and Awards Committee to refund the overpayment of honoraria in the total amount of ₱262,558.25; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to check carefully subsequent claims if BAC is eligible to collect such amount of honoraria pursuant to Section 5 of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005.

We recommended that management instruct the:
Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) to properly plan the PPAs for inclusion in the 20% Development Plan and refrain from charging expenditures that do not partake the nature of capital investments or those not essential or relevant to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes as identified under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 – 1;
MPDC, together with the Municipal Engineer, to abide strictly on the schedule or project implementation period as reflected in the AIP to optimally utilize the fund allocated for the year and to avoid delay in the project execution; and
Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to maintain a separate registry for the utilization of both current and continuing appropriations of PPAs funded under the 20% Development Fund for purposes of proper monitoring.

We recommended that management:
instruct the Budget Officer to (i) appropriate and set aside not less than 5% of the estimated revenue from regular sources for LDRRMF; and (ii) maintain the required separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for QRF, MOOE and CO to facilitate the determination of the unexpended balance of the funds; and
advise the LDDRM Officer to (i) include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan the programs, projects and activities to be undertaken out of the unexpended balance of previous years; (ii) determine calamity-related PPAs and categorize them as to the four thematic areas pursuant to DILG Policy; and (ii) submit report on utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the the Local Chief Executive, in his capacity as Chairman of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) undertake the following courses of actions:
consider the amendment of the composition or reconstitution of the GFPS, including its ExeCom, and create a TWG which shall facilitate the gender mainstreaming efforts of the Municipality through the GAD planning and budgeting process;
adhere strictly to the guidelines on GAD Planning and Budgeting (GPB), particularly, the appropriation of at least five percent (5%) of their total Annual Budget to GAD PPAs and observe the schedule of the preparation of GPB to properly and timely plan, implement and evaluate the PPAs pertaining to the gender mainstreaming efforts of the Municipality to meet the required funding. The Agency may attribute GAD related PPAs in other programs/services provided the attribution made is clearly identified in the GPB; and
prepare and submit the GAD AR based on the GPB to facilitate the Audit Team’s as well as the GFPS’s evaluation of the PPAs implemented out of the GAD Fund.

We commended management for the earnest effort in the prompt remittance of the taxes withheld during the current year as well as the payment of long outstanding trust liabilities to the BIR. Notwithstanding, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant continually take responsibility of the review of the computations of withholding taxes on government money payments and immediately effect the necessary corrections, if any, in compliance with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007.

We recommended that management adopt more effective strategies to collect tax arrears; otherwise, exercise all available remedies set forth in the Local Government Code of 1991, Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality of San Marcelino, and the Market Code to ensure the realization of revenues needed to catalyze progress and development in the Municipality.

We recommended that the MSWD Office (a) ensure that the SEA-K fund assistance be granted only to legitimate members of SKAs/SKGs with priority given to Pantawid Pamilya beneficiaries and see to it that the same were utilized solely for duly approved and intended purposes; (b) encourage SKAs/SKGs officers to exert earnest effort in collecting repayments of the amount granted to the beneficiaries; otherwise, impose the necessary sanctions thereto pursuant to the Saligang Batas; and (c) regularly monitor the implementation of the Sustainable Livelihood Program of the Department of Social Welfare and Development to achieve the noble objective of providing an aggressive credit and savings mobilization to low-income members of the community, thereby promoting the growth of micro-enterprises.
